html:
Exclusive to:  

A Wobster is a catchable sea creature found in the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. Wobsters spawn from Wobster Dens, which house one Wobster each. Wobsters are nocturnal, and will only come out at Dusk, will stay out all through the Night, and will retreat back to their dens when the next day begins. Players who get too close will also cause them to be scared and go back to their dens.

They can be caught using Sea Traps or a Trawl Net. They are attracted to Limpets, which can be used to bait the trap to improve chances of catching them. Leaving them caught in a trap will cause them to starve to death in 1.5 game days, dropping one Rot instead. Wobsters that have been dropped on land by the player will not move and will die after a short amount of time.

When caught alive, they can be used in Crock Pot recipes. As inventory items, they can be murdered by right-clicking them to produce a Dead Wobster. The dead version can only be eaten raw or cooked, and cannot be used in Crock Pot recipes.

Wobsters take 1 blast from the Ice Staff to be frozen and 1 Sleep Dart to be put to sleep.

Dead Wobster

A Dead Wobster is obtained by right-clicking on a live Wobster in the inventory. It can be cooked over a fire, but cannot be used in the Crock Pot.

Delicious Wobster

A Delicious Wobster is obtained from cooking a Wobster at a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea, or Dwarf Star. It cannot be used in the Crock Pot.

Recipe

×2

×2

Fillers cannot be Meats, Fishes (with meat value) or Ice

Tips

  • To restore a live Wobster in the inventory to full freshness, it can be fed any food in the fish or meat category (including Monster Meat and Jellyfish). It can also eat Blubber or Eggs but not any Fruits or Vegetables.

Trivia

  • Being able to only use live Wobsters in the Crock Pot is a reference on how lobsters are often cooked in real life as they are put in boiling water alive.
  • The name, as implied by Wilson's quote, may be a reference to the Looney Tunes character Elmer Fudd's way of speaking, particularly when he refers to Bugs Bunny as a "Wascally Wabbit".
  • Wobsters were introduced to Don't Starve Together in the She Sells Sea Shells update. While the Wobsters retain their name from Shipwrecked, they have a darker coloration, and the two Wobster recipes that were ported from Shipwrecked were renamed as Lobster Bisque and Lobster Dinner.
  • Despite being caught in the same way as other ocean fish, being aquatic, and giving fish value in the crockpot Wobsters do not give Wurt a sanity regen effect for having one in her inventory. However a dead Wobster does give the sanity draining effect from dead fish.

Gallery

  • A Wobster on land.
  • A Wobster in the water.
  • Sleepy Wobsters.
  • Wobsters.
  • Dead Wobsters.

"lobster"
“What a Wascally Wobster.”
–Wilson, when examining a Wobster.
“Come here, you!”
–Willow, when examining a Wobster.
“Wolfgang respects shellbeast's powerful claws.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Wobster.
“It would taste excellent dead and smothered in butter.”
–Wendy, when examining a Wobster.
“ARMORED MEAT SOURCE”
–WX-78, when examining a Wobster.
“A most interesting invertebrate.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Wobster.
“A little melted butter and it'd be delicious.”
–Woodie, when examining a Wobster.
“Now that looks like a proper meal.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Wobster.
“What a complex cephalothorax! I'm going to eat it for dinner!”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Wobster.
“Yöu are dressed för battle, sea-dweller.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Wobster.
“This meat wears armor!”
–Webber, when examining a Wobster.
“Yummy sea bug.”
–Walani, when examining a Wobster.
“Come to me, precious!”
–Warly, when examining a Wobster.
“Don't be scurryin' from me!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Wobster.
“PINCHY MEATS!”
–Wilba, when examining a Wobster.
“Snappy Glub Glub”
–Wormwood, when examining a Wobster.
“Don't mind me, just trying to capture you for my dinner.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Wobster.
A Wobster is a catchable sea creature found in the Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. Wobsters spawn from Wobster Dens, which house one Wobster each. Wobsters are nocturnal, and will only come out at Dusk, will stay out all through the Night, and will retreat back to their dens when the next day begins. Players who get too close will also cause them to be scared and go back to their dens.
They can be caught using Sea Traps or a Trawl Net. They are attracted to Limpets, which can be used to bait the trap to improve chances of catching them. Leaving them caught in a trap will cause them to starve to death in 1.5 game days, dropping one Rot instead. Wobsters that have been dropped on land by the player will not move and will die after a short amount of time.
When caught alive, they can be used in Crock Pot recipes. As inventory items, they can be murdered by right-clicking them to produce a Dead Wobster. The dead version can only be eaten raw or cooked, and cannot be used in Crock Pot recipes.
Wobsters take 1 blast from the Ice Staff to be frozen and 1 Sleep Dart to be put to sleep.

“You should cook up nicely.”
–Wilson, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Edible in a pinch.”
–Willow, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Wolfgang wish he had fearsome hand claws!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“It's met its maker. I've met my lunch.”
–Wendy, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“PROTECTIVE ARMOR HAD LIFEBREAKING BUGS”
–WX-78, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“I might as well eat it now.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“He's thoroughly hooped.”
–Woodie, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Excellent.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“I do not want to experiment with eating raw seafood.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Sleep in Valhalla, armöred öne.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“It's not moving anymore.”
–Webber, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Soon to be good eats.”
–Walani, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“One step closer to my mouth.”
–Warly, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Aye! Now Woodlegs can eat 'em.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“'TIS DEAD”
–Wilba, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Oh. Belly stuff now.”
–Wormwood, when examining a Dead Wobster.
“Your adventure has ended but my dinner has just begun!”
–Wheeler, when examining a Dead Wobster.
A Dead Wobster is obtained by right-clicking on a live Wobster in the inventory. It can be cooked over a fire, but cannot be used in the Crock Pot.

“I can't wait to eat you.”
–Wilson, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Lobster for dinner! Pinch me!”
–Willow, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Is ready to eat now.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“It's a feast!”
–Wendy, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“APPEAL DOES NOT COMPUTE”
–WX-78, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Dinner is served!”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Good eats!”
–Woodie, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“I forgot my bib.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Its muscular cephalothorax makes for some good eatin'.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Yöur armör hides tasty meat.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Yum, yum, yum!”
–Webber, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Now that's good eats!”
–Walani, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Could use garlic-butter...”
–Warly, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Hot meat o'th'sea!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“FWOOSH'DED PINCHY MEATS”
–Wilba, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“Why changed color?”
–Wormwood, when examining a Delicious Wobster.
“A lot of meat on its not bones.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Delicious Wobster.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Wobster là một sinh vật biển có thể bắt được trong Don't Starve: Shipwrecked DLC. Wobsters sinh ra từ Wobster Dens, nơi mỗi Wobster chứa một Wobster. Wobsters là loài sống về đêm và sẽ chỉ ra ngoài vào lúc Chạng vạng, sẽ ở ngoài suốt đêm và sẽ rút lui về hang của chúng khi ngày hôm sau bắt đầu. Người chơi đến quá gần cũng sẽ khiến chúng sợ hãi và quay về hang ổ của mình.
